title: ceraia litoralis
intro: Ceraia litoralis, also known as the sandalwood tree, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, characterized by its fragrant heartwood and oil-rich bark. Traditionally, the plant's bark and roots have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Ceraia litoralis are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reduced pain, improved wound healing, and alleviation of respiratory symptoms. In herbal preparations, Ceraia litoralis is often combined with other plants to create topical creams, salves, and essential oils for skin and respiratory care.
